name: Poha
description: A quick Indian breakfast made with flattened rice, onion, mustard seeds, curry leaves, turmeric, peanuts, green chili, lemon juice, and fresh coriander.
recipeIngredient: 1 to 2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice, to taste
Fresh grated coconut, optional for garnish

itemListElement.text: Rinse the poha gently under running water until softened but not mushy.
Drain well and rest for 5 minutes.
Sprinkle with salt, turmeric powder, and sugar if using. Mix gently and set aside.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add peanuts and fry until lightly golden. Remove and set aside, or leave them in the pan.
Add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add cumin seeds if using, chopped green chili, and curry leaves. Fry briefly until aromatic.
Add chopped onion and sauté until soft and translucent.
If using potato, add diced potato, cover, and cook until tender.
Add the prepared poha to the pan and mix gently.
Cover and steam on low heat for 2 to 3 minutes.
Turn off the heat and add lemon juice, fried peanuts, and chopped coriander.
Mix gently and garnish with fresh grated coconut or sev if desired.
